reasoning: While the fidelity score is high (0.9125), the extremely low programmatic precision (0.4) corroborates the identified concern regarding content duplication. Since the pipeline also flagged the document for review and visual duplication of map metadata can negatively impact screen reader experiences (reading the same info twice), a manual review is necessary per the cost-asymmetry guidelines.

concerns.analysis: There is clear visual duplication of content from page 2 of the PDF. The title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', the date 'April 9, 2025', and the 'Sources' text block are rendered as HTML text. However, these same elements are also fully visible within the map image immediately adjacent to them. This creates a repetitive visual experience where the same information block is displayed twice in succession.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source PDF Page 2 contains a title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', date 'April 9, 2025', and a 'Sources' section as part of the page layout.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot displays the title and date as text, followed by the map image which contains the same title and date, followed again by the 'Sources' text block which is also contained within the image.
concerns.location: HTML section titled 'NC Parcels Project Status by County'

action_items: Assess whether the duplicated text blocks (Title, Date, Sources) should be removed from the HTML or if the image should be marked as decorative/given simplified alt-text to avoid redundancy for screen readers.
